blocks.content: Tuesday. We walked to school the same way we always did but it was different. She fell into step beside me at the corner of Monroe and we didn't make a thing of it.
That was the part I kept turning over. How easy it was. After all the months of almost and not-quite — now it was just easy.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Did you finish the history reading?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I have the rest if you need it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're going to let me copy your notes.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm going to let you borrow my notes. There's a difference.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Borrowing implies you'll understand what you're reading.

She bumped my shoulder with hers and kept walking. I let myself smile, just a little. There are things you wait for and things you think you want and then the real version turns out to be better than all of it. Easier. Like it was always the shape of something that should be there.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You're doing the specific quiet. The one where you're thinking something you're not going to say.
She nodded — small and certain. Like that was enough. Because it was.
